rulings.Case Title: PORTFOLIO RECOVERY ASSOCIATES, LLC VS. CLAYTON SMITH et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 27, 2014, Line 1, DEFENDANT CLAYTON SMITH's MOTION TO SET ASIDE DEFAULT/DEFAULT JUDGMENT/LEAVE TO DEFEND. The motion is denied. Even if the court concludes that several of plaintiff's proffered business records are hearsay and do not meet the requirements of Evidence Code sec. 1271 [business records exception to hearsay rule], defendant still fails to maintain its burden to obtain CCP 473 relief. First, the motion is untimely as to the default. The default was entered on June 4, 2013, which was more than 6 months before defendant filed this motion. (Defendant filed the motion on June 25, 2014). The entry of the default judgment on January 2, 2014, is a separate proceeding. Second, defendant failed to provide a proposed pleading or answer in compliance with CCP 473. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: THE BAUGHMAN COMPANY INC, A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. JOACHIM BERGMANN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Mtn To Compel Re Special Interoggs; To Compel Re: Rfp No 19; To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce And Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 27, 2014, Line 10, DEFENDANTs JOACHIM BERGMANN, ALEX ZWERDLING, BERGMANN ZWERDLING DIRECT's Motion To Compel Re Special Interrogatories; To Compel Re: RFP No 19; To Compel Compliance With Agreement To Produce And Monetary Sanctions. (Part 1 of 2.) Defendants' motions to compel and produce documents are denied in part and granted in part as follows: 1) The motion is denied as to Bergmann Zwerdling Direct Inc.'s Second Set of Special Interrogatories, Nos. 20-221, Bergmann's First Set of Special Interrogatories, Nos. 1-198, and Zwerdling's First Set of Special Interrogatories, Nos. 1-198. The court finds: (a) The moving party failed to sustain its burden as to the necessity for the large number of interrogatories in view of the provisions of C.C.P. Sections 2030.030, 2030.040, and 2030.050. (b) The special interrogatories seek duplicate information. To the extent that the interrogatories concern 66 discrete documents, each document does not require multiple interrogatories concerning the plaintiff's contentions. 2) The motion to compel production of documents in response to Request No. 19 is granted to the extent that plaintiff failed to produce the responsive documents that plaintiff agreed to produce as reflected in the court's prior order of November 15, 2013 (directing further meet and confer discussions). However, if all such documents have already been produced, then plaintiff must alternatively provide a declaration under penalty of perjury certifying that such production has been made. 3) The motion to compel production of documents in response to request numbers 1-4, 5-12, 14-18 and 21-29 is denied in part and granted in part. The motion is denied insofar as the original objections as to breadth and relevance appear well taken, and the correspondence fails to demonstrate that the parties had reached a comprehensive agreement for production of all documents so described. Plaintiff's supplemental responses do not reflect an agreement other than for "non-privileged" documents "if any." The motion is granted to the extent that plaintiff agreed to produce, "communications made by Mr. Baughmann to Plaintiff's actual or potential clients, omitting any copyrighted direct mail pieces attached to any such communications." If all such documents have already been produced, then plaintiff must alternatively provide a declaration under penalty of perjury certifying that such production has been made. 4) The requests for sanctions by both sets of parties are denied. As to the moving parties, the objections were substantially justified. As to the opposing party, sanctions are denied on the ground that imposition of sanctions would otherwise be unjust. Plaintiff's failure to obtain a protective order previously, to move for a proposed protective order, or to provide the court with a trade secrets designation (pursuant to C.C.P. Section 2019.210) limits the court's ability to provide restrictions on discovery or specify alternative discovery methods under C.C.P. Section 2019.030. (Continued to Part 2.) =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: SAN FRANCISCO EMPLOYEES' RETIREMENT SYSTEM VS. ROSA O SERPAS-JONES et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 27 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT ROSA SERPAS-JONES Motion To Compel Responses To Form Interrogatories, Special Interrogatories And Requests For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Peter Catalanotti, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Judge Pro Tem has issued the following tentative ruling: Motion is GRANTED. The request for sanctions is DENIED. CCP § 2023.040 (failure to specify the type of sanctions sought). The prevailing party shall submit a proposed order. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to pcc@manningllp.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CUI XIA RONG VS. ZHI YAN PAN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 27, 2014, Line 8, DEFENDANT CSAA INSURANCE EXCHANG's DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Sustained in part and overruled in part. As to the second cause of action for Violation of the Unruh Act, the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") adequately pleads all of the requisite elements of the cause of action - (1) that defendant denied full and equal services to the plaintiff; (2) that a substantial motivating reason for defendant's conduct was its perception of plaintiff's race; (3) that plaintiff was harmed; and (4) that defendant's conduct was a substantial factor in causing plaintiff's harm. (CACI 3060; FAC, pars. 24-25, 27, 37-39.) The FAC adequately pleads facts establishing that Plaintiffs have standing, as the FAC alleges that they were denied services due to their race. California cases have "allowed parties that are not clients, patrons, or customers in the traditional sense to bring Unruh Act claims." (Strother v. Southern California Permanente Medical Group (9th Cir. 1996) 79 F.3d 859, 873; see e.g. Jackson v. Superior Court (1994) 30 Cal.App.4th 936.) As to the third cause of action for intentional infliction of emotional distress, the demurrer is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The FAC does not plead facts establishing "conduct so extreme as to exceed all bounds of that usually tolerated in a civilized community." (Cochran v. Cochran (1998) 65 Cal.App.4th 488, 494.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: AGESONG LIVING LLC VS. UNIVERSITY MOUND LADIES HOME et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 27, 2014, Line 13, PLAINTIFF AGESONG LIVING LLC's APPLICATION AND HEARING FOR RIGHT TO ATTACH ORDER. Plaintiff's application for a right to attach order and a writ of attachment is granted with modification. First, Plaintiff has shown that the claim is one for which an attachment may be issued since it a claim for money based on an express, unsecured commercial contract-the Management Agreement pursuant to which Plaintiff would manage the senior residential care facility owned and operated by Defendant in exchange for a monthly fee of $15,000. (Dec. of Shabahangi, Ex. A.) Second, the total amount of the alleged breach is readily ascertainable because the Management Agreement expressly provides for the payment of $15,000 per month starting in August 2013, thereby entitling Plaintiff at this point to eleven months of management fees for a total owed of $165,000. However, Plaintiff made no attempt to explain how it reached an estimated amount of $100,000 in attorneys' fees, providing no estimate of necessary hours or the hourly rate to be charged. As such, Plaintiff has failed to satisfy its burden of showing that this amount equals the reasonable attorney fees that will be incurred. Third, Shabahangi's declaration and the accompanying documentary evidence establish the probable validity of Plaintiff's claim that Defendant breached the Management Agreement. Finally, there is nothing in the record to suggest that Plaintiff, in filing the instant application for a writ of attachment, has any purpose other than the recovery on the claim upon which the attachment is based. Thus, the application is granted for the issuance of a writ of attachment in the amount of $165,510. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4:00 p.m. stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if in pro per on the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: FRANKLIN H WRIGHT VS. THE GAP, INC.
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Wednesday, August 27, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT GAP, INC.'s DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. The demurrer to the entire complaint is sustained without leave to amend. Plaintiff did not oppose defendant's demurrer. The court received a copy of plaintiff's motion for judgment on the pleadings and that document appears to be an opposition to defendant's demurrer and motion to strike. The motion for judgment on the pleadings fails to explain how the defective complaint can be cured by amendment. See Goodman v. Kennedy, (1976) 18 Cal.3d 335, 349. The motion to strike is off calendar as moot.
